title: Fitzpatrick's Harbour Town Masterclass: Three-Shot Lead into Sunday
excerpt: Matt Fitzpatrick shot 68 to extend his lead to 17-under at the RBC Heritage, three shots clear of World No. 1 Scottie Scheffler heading into Sunday at Harbour Town.
body:
The English Champion Leads the Way

Matt Fitzpatrick's mastery of Harbour Town Golf Links continues. After shooting 68 on Saturday, the Englishman sits at 17-under par, a commanding three shots clear of World No. 1 Scottie Scheffler heading into Sunday's final round. Fitzpatrick bounced back from a sluggish start to make the crucial moves on the back nine, including a chip-in eagle at the 15th that proved decisive in a crowd-heavy leaderboard.

Fitzpatrick's Score
-17
Lead Over 2nd
3 Shots
Scheffler's Score
-14
RBC Heritage Wins
2

Scheffler's Charge Falls Short

Scottie Scheffler turned in a clinic on Saturday, firing 64 with eight birdies and just one bogey. The World No. 1 erased a seven-shot deficit to threaten Fitzpatrick down the stretch, but the English champion's pair of late eagles — that chip-in at 15 and his brilliant approach into 18 — proved too much to overcome. Scheffler will have his chance Sunday, but Fitzpatrick's experience at Harbour Town (this is his second RBC Heritage title) and his ice-cold putter suggest the job remains uphill for the field.

A Bunched Leaderboard Awaits

This is not a two-man race. Brian Harman posted 63 on Saturday to sit at 13-under, alongside Si Woo Kim and Sepp Straka. Ludvig Aberg, Gary Woodland, Aldrich Potgieter, Patrick Cantlay and Andrew Novak all sit at 12-under, just five shots back. Andrew Novak, defending champion from last year, said it best: "It's a bunched leaderboard. Nobody has really separated yet. I'm going to attack hard and post a number."

Harbour Town in Full Display

The par-4 12th proved pivotal for Fitzpatrick's Saturday — a birdie there restored his solo lead after he’d lost it in the early going. The course’s strategic design, with its island-green 17th and elevated hazards throughout, demands precision over distance. Fitzpatrick’s ball-striking has been surgical. He’s hitting his targets and converting chances when it matters. By contrast, the players chasing him must thread a needle on Sunday to make up ground.•

What's at Stake

A victory here would be Fitzpatrick’s second PGA Tour win of 2026 and his second RBC Heritage title in three years. Following his Valspar Championship victory earlier this season, the 29-year-old is clearly peaking at exactly the right time — the approach to the U.S. Open at Pinehurst in June. Scheffler, for his part, knows the window to get back in the hunt is Sunday’s final 18 holes. Harbour Town has a way of rewarding the patient and punishing the aggressive. That script favours the leader.
